note: uuidv7() (subject, ../../src/harness/session/uuid.ts) calls crypto.getRandomValues; stub feeds deterministic random bytes so the RFC 9562 layout and monotonic-order output are exact. Post-port uuidv7 runs in Rust and won't touch JS global crypto, so this stub won't fire -- Rust shim needs an injectable RNG seam (not one of the four planned seams; OTHER:crypto).

spyOn(Date,'now').mockReturnValue(TIMESTAMP) pins the clock so uuidv7's embedded 48-bit timestamp is asserted exactly (parseTimestamp===TIMESTAMP, and +1 on counter rollover). Date.now is an internal clock dependency of the subject that moves into Rust; the JS spy won't fire post-port, so a ported test must inject now via the clock seam.
